body:
## Issue

Fixes the macOS portion of [#479](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/479) — *"`Not available in latest release` for all desktop apps"*. After PR #490, the maintainer reported (2026-06-17): **"Still no release for macOS"**. The fifth automatic release, `v0.206.0`, confirms it: 6 Linux + 4 Windows + 2 metadata assets = **12 assets, 0 macOS** `.dmg`/`.zip`.

## Root cause (electron-builder 26 signing-skip)

Desktop Release run [`27600376260`](docs/case-studies/issue-479/raw-data/desktop-release-27600376260.json) (the run that built `v0.206.0`) isolates it: `Resolve`, both Linux jobs, both Windows jobs, and the checksum/provenance job **all succeeded** — only the **two macOS jobs failed**, in the pre-upload smoke test with `Mounted app is missing its signed CodeResources envelope`.

The PR #490 reseal hook was **never reached**. The macOS log goes straight from `• packaging platform=darwin arch=x64` → `• no event listeners found event=afterSign` → `• building target=macOS zip` — **no signing line, and the hook's `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG` output never appears** ([macos-x64 log](docs/case-studies/issue-479/ci-logs/desktop-release-27600376260-macos-x64.log) L1109-1130, error at L1281).

electron-builder **26** changed the control flow: `MacPackager.sign()` → `findSigningIdentity()` returns `null` for **any** qualifier other than `"-"` when no Apple certificate is present (even with `CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY=false`), and `isSignAllowed()` then **skips signing entirely — never invoking the custom `mac.sign` hook**. Under electron-builder **25** (what `konard/vk-bot-desktop` uses — hence "just copy the build logic" worked there) the hook still ran without the flag. PRs #487/#490 added and hardened the reseal hook, but on EB26 it was dead code because the flag that activates it was missing.

## Fix

One mandatory flag on the ad-hoc build command in `.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml`:

```
-c.mac.identity=-
```

With it, `findSigningIdentity()` resolves the ad-hoc identity, EB26 invokes `./scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.cjs`, and the existing reseal + verify logic produces the `Contents/_CodeSignature/CodeResources` envelope the smoke test requires. The flag takes effect once merged to `main`; the next auto-release (or a manual `workflow_dispatch` for `v0.206.0`) self-heals the missing macOS assets via the idempotency guard (the guard requires all 14 desktop assets, so a partial release rebuilds).

## Reproduction

`electron-builder --mac --publish never -c.mac.sign=./sign.cjs` on EB26 with no Apple cert → the hook never runs and the `.app` has no `_CodeSignature/CodeResources`. Adding `-c.mac.identity=-` makes the hook run. Cannot be reproduced locally on Linux (no macOS DMG build), so the fix is grounded in reading electron-builder 26 source (`macPackager.ts`, `MacTargetHelper.ts`, `macCodeSign.ts`) and the live CI logs above.

## Tests

`tests/unit/ci-cd/release_publishing.rs` now asserts `-c.mac.identity=-` is present on the ad-hoc command (without it, signing is skipped and the `.app` ships unsigned). All 5 desktop release-publishing tests pass:

```
test ci_cd::release_publishing::desktop_macos_package_uses_vk_bot_signing_configuration ... ok
test ci_cd::release_publishing::desktop_release_workflow_signs_and_smoke_tests_macos_artifacts ... ok
test ci_cd::release_publishing::desktop_linux_deb_package_metadata_is_configured ... ok
test ci_cd::release_publishing::desktop_release_lets_electron_builder_read_package_json_build_key ... ok
test ci_cd::release_publishing::desktop_linux_artifact_names_are_normalized_for_download_contract ... ok
```

## Case study & cross-repo audit

- **Case study** updated in [`docs/case-studies/issue-479/`](docs/case-studies/issue-479/README.md) — new §0.6 documents the EB26 signing-skip root cause with the `v0.206.0` release inventory, run `27600376260` metadata, and both macOS job logs (moved into `ci-logs/`).
- **Template comparison** ([`template-comparison/REPORT.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-479/template-comparison/REPORT.md)) updated: the EB26 signing-skip is **absent from all four templates** — none uses electron-builder or signs macOS bundles (the only Electron packaging, `js/example-app.yml`, uses **Electron Forge** and does no signing). So there is **no upstream bug to file** for this defect; the report records a ready-to-file reproducible example + workaround + fix for the optional desktop-release upstreaming enhancement.

## CI/CD false-positive check

The recent "skipped" Desktop Release runs are **correct behavior**, not false positives: they were triggered by PR-branch CI completions, which the `workflow_run.head_branch == 'main'` guard intentionally ignores. Linux + Windows release publishing already works (confirmed at `v0.206.0`); this PR closes the remaining macOS gap.

## Web structure (verified, unchanged)

Confirmed intact from prior PRs: `/` (landing chooser with the big `.source-cta` source-code hero button), `/app`, `/download` (all platforms, with the **real** macOS Gatekeeper screenshots from `konard/vk-bot-desktop`, validated as genuine PNGs), `/docs/` hub, and `/docs/api` (rustdoc). Covered by `tests/e2e/tests/issue-479.spec.js` and `issue-479-site.spec.js`.



Fixes #479

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/479

electron-builder 26's MacPackager.sign() calls findSigningIdentity(), which
returns null -- skipping signing entirely and never invoking the custom
mac.sign hook -- unless a real certificate is found OR mac.identity is exactly
"-". With no Apple secrets the produced .app had no _CodeSignature, so the
pre-upload smoke test failed with "missing its signed CodeResources envelope"
and macOS assets (.dmg/.zip) were never published to releases.

Pass -c.mac.identity=- (plus FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG=1 for diagnostics) so
electron-builder reaches doSign() and runs the ad-hoc hook.

Refs #479
commits.messageHeadline: fix(desktop-release): pass mac.identity=- so EB26 invokes ad-hoc signing
docs(case-study): add PR #510 EB26 signing-skip root cause + v0.206.0…
docs(template-comparison): document EB26 macOS signing-skip is absent…
docs(case-study): add run 27600376260 macOS failure logs (force-added…

title: fix(desktop-release): pass mac.identity=- so electron-builder 26 signs macOS ad-hoc builds (#479)
